[2019-04-22 17:42] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): hahaha, you assumed I anticipated that and gave me the answer I was looking for.
[2019-04-22 17:42] jamberine: do we actually look at units in base+1? No one's ever said tatsu is underpowered since it loses base+1
[2019-04-22 17:44] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): We don't do that much lately (or at all), I just didn't want to exclude that part of the analysis since some still do.
[2019-04-22 17:44] jamberine: I don't think simpson's paradox can apply here since it comes up when one of the groups is subjected to harder things more often, and units are completely random
[2019-04-22 17:47] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): care to elaborate jamberine? I might not be understanding the simpson's paradox
[2019-04-22 17:48] jamberine: the reason the women looked worse in the example despite being better in every category is because they chose the more competitive departments
[2019-04-22 17:49] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Trying to 1 for 1 some of the questions asked under the 'video game' section, could we say low tier vs high is a factor? what about heavy blue vs green vs red sets?
[2019-04-22 17:49] jamberine: similarly for the kidney stones treatment A was applied to the generally less successful large stones more often
[2019-04-22 17:52] mrguy888: Vivid drone was a perfect example of simpsons paradox, @Zera The winrates for the unit were pretty even for p1 and p2 and that was cited as a reason to not change it. The problem was that the important statistics was p1 and p2 winrates in individual sets. The players all knew that most sets were brutally unfair but they were unfair for p1 and p2 at a fairly even rate.
[2019-04-22 17:53] jamberine: I would argue that that isn't simpson's paradox at all
[2019-04-22 17:53] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Interesting, do we have information on what those sets were @mrguy888 ?
[2019-04-22 17:54] jamberine: not that it actaully matters what you call it
[2019-04-22 17:54] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): why do you say it's not simpson's paradox jamborine? again, just trying to undestand
[2019-04-22 17:55] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): on Vivids, what made it unfair in favor of p1, for example? was it a specific type of unit or what?
[2019-04-22 17:56] mrguy888: I have written extensively on both vivid and wild drone, go look up one of those many times.
[2019-04-22 17:56] mtanzer: it seems like a clear example of simpson's paradox to me.  from wikipedia, "a trend appears in several different groups of data but disappears or reverses when these groups are combined"

group 1: sets with vivid that favor p1
group 2: sets with vivid that favor p2
trend: one player is favored

after combining it seems neither player is favored
[2019-04-22 17:57] jamberine: I understand the trend to refer to one player
[2019-04-22 17:57] jamberine: otherwise "reverses" makes no sense
[2019-04-22 17:59] jamberine: also that example doesn't seem paradoxical at all
[2019-04-22 17:59] mrguy888: Well Simpon's paradox isn't actually a paradox either.
[2019-04-22 17:59] jamberine: yes but that example doesn't seem to be an apparent paradox either
[2019-04-22 17:59] jamberine: there are some situations where one side is favored and some where the other is favored and on aggregate neither is favored
[2019-04-22 18:00] jamberine: simpson's paradox is at least apparently paradoxical
[2019-04-22 18:01] jamberine: in situation 1, A is favored, in situation 2, A is favored, but B is favored on aggregate
[2019-04-22 18:01] mtanzer: yeah I see what you're saying.  hmm maybe the vivid example isn't simpson's after all
[2019-04-22 18:02] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Tried to look for the vivid content, but couldn't find anything concrete. it's either scattered comments or conclusions without details or background, thanks anyways.
[2019-04-22 18:03] mtanzer: the old version of vivid had similar problems to the old version of wild.  too inflexible for early turns left openings too forced
[2019-04-22 18:04] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I guess I'll never get it...
[2019-04-22 18:04] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): @Terra Reveene what does `W9: hhebcy ns 13plg 3f2aw` mean?
[2019-04-22 18:04] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I'm reading the article but it doesn't seem to translate it.
[2019-04-22 18:05] mrguy888: It's obviously notation for p1's 9th move.
[2019-04-22 18:06] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I thought so, but then Elyot says: B11: gg wp
[2019-04-22 18:06] mtanzer: jeacaveo just imagine a world where drone costs 7.  it seems obvious that this favors p2 because the high price leaves one player with a way more efficient opening than the other.  old versions of vivid and wild had this sort of problem
[2019-04-22 18:06] mrguy888: Okay that one is super easy to translate
[2019-04-22 18:07] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Oh, W is for white and B is for black, nvm. The rest is the intriguing part
[2019-04-22 18:07] mrguy888: Give it a little thought jeaco
[2019-04-22 18:07] mrguy888: You can even google that last one
[2019-04-22 18:09] mrguy888: I believe everything you guys are writing about vivid falls under what he called "conclusions without details"
[2019-04-22 18:09] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): this is at least a layer below and makes it clearer.
[2019-04-22 18:10] mrguy888: Well I have said that many times.
[2019-04-22 18:12] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): not denying it... a search in this sever for messages were you mentioned 'vivid drone' only gets 5. messages were you mentioned 'vivid', gets 25 (most of which were about breaching vivid vs tarsier).
[2019-04-22 18:13] mrguy888: The first page of reddit has a wild drone thread where I explained all of it.
[2019-04-22 18:13] mrguy888: The discord is pretty new compared to reddit.
[2019-04-22 18:13] zera777 (Zera): It's better to kill Vivid over Tarsier in a breach, right?
[2019-04-22 18:14] mrguy888: I mean if the game is close then their tarsier is killing a vivid too, right?
[2019-04-22 18:14] mrguy888: And if the game is not close then it doesn't matter.
[2019-04-22 18:17] zera777 (Zera): Oh no, it has to matter. I figure, if you have defense, you should kill vivid, because 3 gold can buy opponent *at least* one point of soak per turn, whereas a Tarsier can do *at most* one damage per turn.
[2019-04-22 18:18] amalloy: @Zera the point is, if you have defense and you're choosing whether to breach tarsier or vivid, you win no matter what choice you make
[2019-04-22 18:18] mrguy888: That isn't true amalloy.
[2019-04-22 18:18] mrguy888: You can lose games for killing vivid instead of tarsier.
